briefs.title: Burnaby Needs a Chief Election Officer for the 2026 Local Election
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby needs to appoint a Chief Election Officer to run the 2026 General Local Election. This person will be in charge of making sure the election runs smoothly and according to the rules.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
1.
CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

briefs.title: North Road BIA Renewal Approved in Burnaby for 2026
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA helps local businesses work together to improve their area. The renewal means the BIA will continue its work.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.2
CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL
1.
Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal 2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.
see Bylaw #14797

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Council Approval Needed for 2026 Corporate Matters
briefs.summary: The Burnaby City Council needs to approve some yearly corporate tasks for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) for 2026. These tasks help keep the BHA running smoothly.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026
1.
BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget Released
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has released its financial statements for 2025 and its operating budget for 2026. These documents outline the BHA's financial performance and planned spending for the upcoming year.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026 OPERATING BUDGET
1.
BHA - 2026 Financial Statements 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - BHA - 2025 Audited Financial Statements 2026-04-14 .pdf
3.
Attachment 2 - BHA - 2026 Operating Budget 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To present the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Allow E-Bike Share, Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Programs
briefs.summary: Burnaby is updating its bylaws to allow e-bike share programs and electric kick scooter pilot programs within the city. These changes will affect where and how these devices can be used on streets and in parks. The goal is to manage these new transportation options safely.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaws to understand where e-bikes and e-scooters are permitted.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM
1.
Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose:
To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report Due to BC Ministry of Housing
briefs.summary: Burnaby must send a Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This is required by the Housing Supply Act (a law to increase housing) and its rules. The report shows how Burnaby is meeting its housing targets.
briefs.action_required: Submit Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT
1.
Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report - 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Provincial Housing Target Order 2026-04-14.pdf
3.
Attachment 2 - Housing Target Progress Report Form 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose:
To present the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the
Housing Supply Act
and Housing Supply Regulation.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC Seeks Participation Authorization for Year 7
briefs.summary: Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) is seeking authorization to participate in Year 7. BBBC is a program, but the document does not specify what the program is or what changes are being made.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
BUILDING BENCHMARK BC
1.
Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Building Benchmark BC Annual Report 2024 (Year 5) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose:
To update Council on Building Benchmark BC and seek authorization to participate in Year 7.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Fortis BC Right-of-Way on Pandora and Gaglardi
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a statutory right-of-way (SRW) for FortisBC. This gives FortisBC the legal right to install, operate, and maintain natural gas equipment on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY - BURNABY MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY FOR FORTIS BC
1.
7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way - Burnaby Mountain Fortis SRW 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Four-Plex Development Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract to develop a modular four-plex housing unit. This is a pilot project to explore new housing options.

key_dates.label: Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final Date

briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX AT 8315 14TH AVENUE
1.
CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project
.

briefs.title: Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The local government approved a contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. This means a contractor will be hired to replace the culvert.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CA - (CB-8039) JERRY ROGERS CREEK CULVERT REPLACEMENT
1.
CA - CB-8039 Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a contract for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. This project involves replacing culverts (tunnels carrying water under a road or path) and paving work along the Greenway.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CA - (CB-3264) CENTRAL VALLEY GREENWAY CULVERT REPLACEMENT AND PAVING WORKS
1.
CA - (CB-3264) Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project.

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Services Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a change to the contract for cellular voice and data services. This ensures the city continues to have reliable communication services for its operations.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
CC - TELEPHONY - CELLULAR VOICE AND DATA SERVICES
1.
CC - Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Contract History (Telephony) 2026-04-14 .pdf
3.
Attachment 2 - Council Report dated September 25, 2023 (Telephony) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services.

briefs.title: HVAC System Maintenance Contract Change Approved for City Buildings
briefs.summary: A contract change for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance and repair services at city-owned buildings has been approved. This ensures these systems continue to be maintained.
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CC - HVAC SYSTEM MAINTENANCE & REPAIR SERVICES
1.
CC - HVAC System Maint. and Repair Services 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Contract History (HVAC System) 2026-04-14 .pdf
3.
Attachment 2 - Previous Council Report dated May 27, 2025 (HVAC System) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a contract change (CC) for HVAC system maintenance & repair services at various City-owned buildings.

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The council approved renewing the contract for the Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription. This ensures continued access to the WebEx Contact Center platform. The contract is for an unspecified term.
briefs.action_required: Review the contract details to understand the terms and conditions.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - CISCO WEBEX CONTACT CENTER LICENSE SUBSCRIPTION
1.
CR - Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscriptions 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Contract History (Cisco) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription.

briefs.title: Fire Protection System Maintenance Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The City Council approved the renewal of a contract for fire protection systems and equipment installation, maintenance, and repair services. This ensures these services will continue to be provided.
briefs.source_body:
7.15
CR - FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEMS AND EQUIPMENT INSTALLATION MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR SERVICES
1.
CR – Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation Mtnce and Repair Services 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Contract History (Fire Services) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation Maintenance and Repair Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the wording and content for a plaque. The plaque will commemorate the opening of the Burnaby Lake Overpass.
briefs.source_body:
7.16
BURNABY LAKE OVERPASS OFFICIAL OPENING PLAQUE
1.
Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for the wording and content for the commemorative plaque to be installed as part of the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Review Financial Practices to Avoid Tax Hikes
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will hire an external reviewer. This reviewer will check the city's financial practices. The goal is to protect the city's money and prevent big tax increases or higher service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES
1.
FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning Application for 20-Storey Apartment on Beresford Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider forwarding a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street to First and Second Reading at a future council meeting. The development is planned under the Royal Oak Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Review the rezoning application documents if you have concerns.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET - PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
1.
PDC- REZ 25-11 5053 Beresford Street 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - REZ 25- 11 - Part 1 of 3 - Sketch 1 and 2 2026-04-14.pdf
3.
Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 2 of 3 - Shadow Study, Site Plan and Subdivision Plan 2026-04-14.pdf
4.
Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 3 of 3 - Front Elevation, West and East Elevations 2026-04-14.pdf
5.
Attachment 2 - REZ 25-11 - Development Statistics 2026-04-14.pdf
6.
Attachment 3 - REZ 25-11 - Rezoning Prerequisites 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Riverbend Business Park Amenity Pier: Developer Forfeits Security Deposit
briefs.summary: The developer of 8351 Fraser Reach Court will not build the planned public amenity pier. Instead, they will forfeit (give up) the security deposit they had provided to the City of Burnaby. The City will hold this money and decide later how it will be used.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
1.
Amenity Pier Covenant  Developer Election to Forfeit Security 2026-04-14.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security pending future Council direction.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw Rejected
briefs.summary: A proposed bylaw to designate the Burnaby North Road area as a Business Improvement Area (BIA) will not proceed. Sufficient petitions against the BIA were received, halting its creation. A BIA allows local businesses to collectively fund improvements and promotions in their area.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Hotel & Accommodation
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
1.
14797 Burnaby BIA (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
see Item 7.2.
NOTE: sufficient petitions against the proposed BIA have been received
